spec_match.rationale: Models the spec rule that both source_debug_key and trigger_debug_key must be present, both ar_debug cookies must be set, and the report must not cross the app/web boundary before debug keys serialize.

interactive.rationale: Cookie selects, a context select, a debug-key registration-shape select, and validated key inputs drive the live debug-info tuple and serialized report.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggle cookie state, watch debug keys get stripped — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Partial source-only, trigger-only, neither-key, blank key, non-numeric key, oversized key, missing-cookie, and cross-app/web stripping paths are covered; verbose debug reports and reporting-origin/cookie-origin differences remain out of scope.

design_system.rationale: All CSS uses design-system variables, disabled key inputs retain readable contrast, and the report is rendered without innerHTML.

openQuestions.title: Add a verbose-debug-report counterfactual
openQuestions.detail: Attribution Reporting also has verbose debug reports (separate from the attribution report itself) which have their own debug-key inclusion rules. Either mention they exist or add a third report panel that emits the verbose-debug shape so the reader knows the change applies to two report types.

summary: Chrome 148 DevTools verification: the debug-key registration-shape select now covers both-key, source-only, trigger-only, and neither-key states; one-sided or invalid debug keys serialize neither debug key and show the distinct reason, while both valid keys plus both cookies in same context still include both keys. Current feature conformance route reports 5 pass / 0 fail with no console messages.
